title: Marlow & Vane property flyer batch: grade listing photos, vectorize the agency crest, and InDesign data-merge a listings CSV into per-property print flyers (PDF + high-res JPEG)
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: "We are seeking a talented designer to create a real estate flyer for a new listing. The flyer should be visually appealing and effectively communicate the property's features and benefits." REASONABLY ENRICHED into a runnable Adobe-only data-merge benchmark: the raw brief implies a from-scratch flyer layout, which is NOT in connector scope (there is no layout composition; document_merge_data_layout / document_render_layout only BIND/EXPORT a GENUINE authored .indd). So the client (a fictional boutique agency, Marlow & Vane Realty) is modeled as supplying the authored InDesign flyer template carrying real <<fields>> plus photo frames, a listings roster CSV (one row per new listing), the raw agent-shot property photos, and the flat agency crest PNG — a normal freelancer hand-off / recurring listing-flyer engagement. The Adobe chain then does the genuinely-doable heavy lifting the brief rewards: grading and consistently cropping the dull/tilted property photos so each listing reads well, vectorizing the crest for crisp print, sourcing a subtle paper/linen texture, and running a REAL InDesign data-merge so each roster row binds into the template to batch-produce one flyer per property, exported as a print-ready PDF and high-res JPEGs. "New listing" is honored: the roster holds real per-listing fields (price, beds, baths, sqft, address, blurb).
one_line_ask: Grade four raw property photos, vectorize the Marlow & Vane crest, source a subtle linen texture, then run the client's authored InDesign flyer template through a data-merge against a four-listing roster CSV to batch-produce a print-ready PDF and per-listing high-res JPEGs.
full_brief: Marlow & Vane Realty, a boutique residential agency, needs its new-listing flyer sheet finished for print and email. Our marketing lead has handed off an authored InDesign flyer template (listing_flyer_template.indd) — a US Letter portrait one-listing-per-page layout in a clean editorial style with a hero photo frame, three thumbnail frames, a crest placement, a full-bleed background frame for a subtle paper/linen texture, and REAL data-merge fields (<<address>>, <<price>>, <<beds>>, <<baths>>, <<sqft>>, <<blurb>>, <<agent_name>>, <<agent_phone>>). We are also handing off a listings roster CSV (four current new listings, one row each with those exact field names), four raw agent-shot property photos (a curb-appeal exterior, a bright living room, a kitchen, a primary bedroom), and the agency crest as a flat PNG. The photos are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull/flat, cool white-balance, and slightly under- or over-exposed by window light. The job: (1) make every property photo look bright, warm and inviting — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance toward a welcoming daylight look, gently lift shadows, push vibrance a touch (never garish — this is real estate, not a burger), and crop all four consistently to the frame ratios the template expects (a 3:2 hero and matching thumbnails); (2) vectorize the flat crest PNG into a clean SVG so the monogram stays razor-sharp at large print size; (3) license a subtle paper/linen texture from Adobe Stock to back the layout without competing with the photos; (4) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through InDesign data-merge against the roster CSV so every listing's address / price / beds / baths / sqft / blurb / agent contact binds into its own flyer page, with the graded photos, crest and texture placed; (5) export the bound multi-page document to a print-ready PDF (all four flyers) and also emit a high-resolution JPEG per listing for email and MLS use; (6) write a short recipe note recording the grade settings and the CSV-to-field mapping so the same treatment can be re-run next week when new listings come in. Deliverable dimensions: US Letter portrait (8.5 x 11 in), ~300 DPI print. Brand constraints: keep the editorial/clean look, do not oversaturate, crest must be crisp vector, agent contact block bound from the CSV per row.

inputs.name: listing_flyer_template.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a US Letter portrait (8.5x11in) one-listing-per-page real-estate flyer in a clean editorial style. Must contain a hero image frame sized 3:2, three matching thumbnail frames, a crest placement frame, a full-bleed background frame for a paper/linen texture, and REAL InDesign data-merge placeholder fields named <<address>>, <<price>>, <<beds>>, <<baths>>, <<sqft>>, <<blurb>>, <<agent_name>>, <<agent_phone>> laid out as a listing card (headline address, price band, feature row, descriptive blurb, agent contact footer). Authored by hand in InDesign so the data-merge fields actually bind and paginate one page per roster row; a PDF-converted .indd with literal text would NOT bind.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the agency's own draft template (a normal freelancer revision/recurring-engagement hand-off). document_merge_data_layout is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .indd carrying real merge fields — it cannot be connector-generated, so it is listed as a supplied input, not a chain output.

inputs.name: listings_roster.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns address,price,beds,baths,sqft,blurb,agent_name,agent_phone holding FOUR fictional current listings for a boutique agency (mix of a craftsman bungalow, a mid-century ranch, a downtown loft condo, and a suburban colonial). Realistic US addresses on fictional streets, list prices $415,000-$1,275,000, beds 1-4, baths 1-3.5, sqft 720-3,200, a one-line appetizing blurb per listing, and one of two fictional agents (Dana Marlow / Theo Vane) with fictional phone numbers. Column names must exactly match the InDesign merge fields <<address>>,<<price>>,<<beds>>,<<baths>>,<<sqft>>,<<blurb>>,<<agent_name>>,<<agent_phone>>.
inputs.realism_notes: One row per new listing so the [T] data-merge paginates one flyer page per row; headers match the authored .indd merge fields exactly so binding succeeds.

inputs.name: photo_exterior_curb.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a craftsman bungalow front exterior on an overcast afternoon, slightly tilted horizon (about 3 degrees), flat dull grey lighting, cool/blue white balance, a parked car and a trash bin partly in frame at the edge, lawn slightly washed out. Real siding texture, real shadows, candid real-estate-agent phone photo, no HDR gloss, no CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately imperfect (tilt, flat cool tone, overcast) so the grade+straighten+crop chain has real work; edge clutter justifies a crop, not object removal. gpt-image-2 for photoreal architecture.

inputs.name: photo_living_room.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a bright living room interior with a large window, harsh window backlight blowing out the glass while the room interior sits underexposed and cool, slightly crooked framing, a stray power cable visible near the baseboard. Real furniture, real hardwood floor, candid listing photo, no virtual staging, no CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Backlit window + underexposed interior justifies auto-tone, shadow-lift, warm-up and exposure steps; crooked framing justifies straighten.

inputs.name: photo_kitchen.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a mid-century kitchen, dull yellow-green fluorescent tube lighting giving an unpleasant cast, flat contrast, slightly tilted, cluttered countertop edge with a dish rack in frame. Real cabinets, real countertop texture, candid agent photo, no CGI gloss.
inputs.realism_notes: Ugly fluorescent cast justifies color-temperature correction + single-color desaturation of the green cast; tilt justifies straighten.

inputs.name: photo_primary_bedroom.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a primary bedroom, dim evening lighting, muddy underexposed shadows, cool white balance, slightly crooked, a laundry basket partly visible at the frame edge. Real bedding texture, real window blinds, candid listing photo, no CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Underexposed + cool justifies auto-tone/exposure/warm-up; crooked + edge clutter justifies straighten + consistent crop.

inputs.name: marlow_vane_crest.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat monogram crest logo reading 'M&V' with 'MARLOW & VANE REALTY' beneath it in a refined serif, a simple thin-line shield outline, solid dark-navy on a transparent background, no gradients, high contrast, crisp flat raster PNG suitable for vectorizing.
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image for clean legible logo text; flat high-contrast solid navy so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG (no gradients to trip up the trace).

outputs.name: MarlowVane_Listing_Flyers_print.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready multi-page US Letter portrait (8.5x11in) PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign document — one flyer page per roster listing; per-row copy (address/price/beds/baths/sqft/blurb/agent) bound from the CSV; graded photos + vectorized crest + linen texture placed; ~300 DPI print quality.

outputs.name: MarlowVane_Listing_JPEGs_x4
outputs.spec: Four high-resolution JPEGs, one per listing flyer page (US Letter portrait, max quality), for email blasts and MLS upload.

outputs.name: graded_property_photos_x4
outputs.spec: Four color-graded, straightened, consistently cropped property photos (exterior 3:2 hero + living room / kitchen / bedroom thumbnails) matching the template frames, with a shared warm/bright editorial look.

outputs.name: marlow_vane_crest.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the Marlow & Vane monogram crest for crisp scaling at large print size.

outputs.name: flyer_recipe_note.txt
outputs.spec: A short recipe note recording the per-photo grade settings (straighten, auto-tone, temperature, shadow-lift, vibrance, crop ratios) and the CSV-column-to-InDesign-field mapping, so the same flyer batch can be re-run for next week's listings.

connector_workflow.note: Search Adobe Stock for a subtle paper/linen background texture to back the flyer without competing with the photos (low-res candidates).
License and download the chosen linen texture at full-res (license BEFORE use); becomes the layout background asset.
Level the tilted horizon on the curb-appeal exterior hero shot.
Auto-tone the straightened exterior to fix the flat/dull overcast exposure.
Warm the cool/blue overcast white balance toward an inviting daylight look.
Gently lift the crushed shadows so the shaded siding and lawn read cleanly.
Push vibrance a touch so the lawn/sky read fresh — restrained, not garish (real estate look).
Crop/resize to the 3:2 hero frame the template expects, trimming the parked car / trash bin at the edge (crop, not object removal).
Auto-tone the backlit living room to recover the underexposed interior (same grade pipeline).
Nudge exposure up on the dim interior so the room is bright and welcoming.
Warm the cool interior to match the exterior hero's white balance for a consistent set.
Crop the living room to the matching 3:2 thumbnail frame, trimming the stray power cable at the baseboard edge.
Straighten the tilted kitchen shot.
Auto-tone the flat fluorescent-lit kitchen.
Correct the ugly yellow-green fluorescent cast toward neutral daylight.
Knock down the lingering green cast by desaturating just the green channel so cabinets read true.
Crop the kitchen to the 3:2 thumbnail frame, trimming the cluttered dish-rack countertop edge.
Auto-tone the dim underexposed primary bedroom.
Warm the cool bedroom to the consistent inviting look.
Crop the bedroom to the matching 3:2 thumbnail frame, trimming the laundry basket at the edge so all four photos share the frame ratio.
Vectorize the flat crest PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp large-print scaling.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: exterior_hero_3x2, living_thumb_3x2, kitchen_thumb_3x2, bedroom_thumb_3x2, crest_svg, linen_texture_fullres
connector_workflow.note: Initialize an upload session for all prepped flyer assets (graded photos, vector crest, texture).
Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs the InDesign template/data-merge will reference.
Create a Creative Cloud project folder to hold the flyer batch inputs and outputs for the recurring engagement.

InDesign data-merge: bind the four roster listings (address/price/beds/baths/sqft/blurb/agent) into the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>), paginating one flyer page per row; graded photos + crest + texture sit in their frames. [T] requires the user-authored template.
Export the merged multi-page InDesign document to a print-ready ~300 DPI US Letter PDF (all four flyers).
Export the same merged document to high-resolution per-page JPEGs (one per listing) for email/MLS use.
Copy the exported PDF + JPEGs into the project folder so the whole flyer batch lives together for hand-off.
Write a recipe note recording each photo's grade settings and the CSV-column-to-InDesign-field mapping so the batch can be re-run for next week's listings.

chaining_note: Strict output-to-input chaining throughout. The exterior hero runs a 6-link grade pipeline: photo_exterior_curb.jpg -> auto_straighten(3) -> auto_tone(4) -> color_temperature(5) -> dark_portions/shadow-lift(6) -> vibrance(7) -> crop(8), each step consuming only the prior step's output. The living room / kitchen / bedroom photos each feed their own grade->crop sub-chains (9-12, 13-17, 18-20); the kitchen adds a temperature-then-single-color-desaturation branch to kill the fluorescent green (15->16). The linen texture chains search(1)->license(2). The crest chains raw PNG->vectorize(21). All six prepped outputs (exterior_hero_3x2, living/kitchen/bedroom_thumb_3x2, crest_svg, linen_texture_fullres) converge into the upload session(22)->finalize(23)->create project folder(24)->data-merge(25), which also consumes the authored listing_flyer_template.indd + roster CSV. The merged multi-page .indd(25) is the single source for both exports — render PDF(26) and render per-page JPEGs(27) — which then converge into asset_copy_assets(28) into the project folder, and the four graded photos + merged doc feed the local recipe note(29). Nothing after the step-1 search and the raw input loads originates outside the chain; the flyer batch emerges only from the assembled chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 29 connector calls across four tool groups (image_* raster grading, asset_* stock/storage/folders, document_* InDesign data-merge+render, local recipe note), 17 distinct Adobe tools — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running four separate property photos through consistent but photo-specific grade pipelines (the kitchen needs an extra fluorescent-cast neutralize + green-desaturate branch; the living room needs an exposure lift for the backlit window), vectorizing the crest, sourcing+licensing stock, then converging everything into a GENUINE InDesign [T] data-merge that paginates one flyer per roster row, dual-exporting a multi-page PDF and per-page JPEGs, filing them into a project folder, and emitting a re-run recipe. It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / expand / object-removal-with-reconstruction / text-to-image / from-scratch layout / upscaling; edge clutter (cars, cables, laundry) is handled by CROPPING, not inpainting; image_fill_area is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout / document_render_layout only BIND/EXPORT the client's authored .indd, which is a supplied input; vibrance is kept restrained per the real-estate brand constraint; real per-listing copy comes from the roster CSV.

reverify.banned_capability_check: None of the banned capabilities are used. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / generative-fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo (the crest is a SUPPLIED PNG, only vectorized); no upscaling/super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction/inpainting (edge clutter — parked car, trash bin, power cable, dish rack, laundry basket — is removed by CROPPING to the template frame, never by reconstructing occluded pixels); no bespoke font/letterform design; no unreachable external account. image_fill_area is NOT used at all. The layout is not composed by the connector: document_merge_data_layout [T] BINDS the client's authored listing_flyer_template.indd (a supplied input carrying real <<fields>>), and document_render_layout [C] only EXPORTS the merged doc — neither composes a new layout. A PDF-converted .indd would not bind, and the spec/input notes state the template must be genuinely authored.
reverify.notes: Risky-looking steps stay in-bounds: (1) image_vectorize (n21) traces a SUPPLIED flat high-contrast PNG crest into SVG — a legitimate raster->vector trace, not logo generation. (2) image_adjust_single_color_saturation (n16) desaturates the green channel to fight a fluorescent cast — a global HSL-style adjustment, no masking/compositing claim. (3) image_crop_and_resize (n8,12,17,20) removes frame-edge clutter by cropping to the template ratio, which is honest — no occluded content is reconstructed. (4) asset_license_and_download_stock (n2) licenses BEFORE use. (5) document_merge_data_layout is correctly [T] with the authored .indd + matching-header CSV as supplied inputs; per-listing copy is REAL roster data, honoring the 'new listing' brief. (6) local_write_recipe_note (n29) is [L], server='local', emitting a re-run note — an in-palette local step. Counts verified: 29 connector_workflow steps -> tool_call_count=29; 17 distinct adobe-server tools (local_write_recipe_note excluded) -> distinct_adobe_tools=17 (>=6); >=10 calls -> qualifies as long-horizon flagship. SLOT-MATCH confirmed: category=datamerge_print / operation-family Layout & Data (O5) / output_modality document / workflow_nature=create all equal slot L06; vertical real_estate_property matches; fictional IP-clean brand (Marlow & Vane Realty) replaces the anonymous real client. workflow_nature=create: a NEW deliverable (per-listing flyer batch) assembled from SUPPLIED parts (authored template, roster, photos, crest) via data-merge, not generative.
